@gmacbm posted on 2022-12-23T01:47:27:

4# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues:Divine Knock-out
- Steam AppID of the game:1294660

## System Information
- GPU: AMD
- Driver/LLVM version: Steamdeck
- Kernel version: <!-- e.g. 4.17 -->
- Proton version:7.0

## I confirm:
- [ ] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [ ] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms:
The dko game easy anti cheat engine using steamdeck is working fine with steam os 3.3 version using proton but upon upgrading to steam os 3.4 with proton the easy anti cheat engine is not loading anymore which prevent the game in launching.


## Reproduction

1.  `Divine Knock out DKO` the AppID is 1294660
2. Linux version: Steam os 3.4
3. Steamdeck device
4. Easy anti cheat engine prevent the game from launching.

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Envelope
- Steam AppID of the game: 2717480

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 7900 GRE
- Video driver version: Mesa 24.2.0-devel
- Kernel version: 6.9.8-200.fc40.x86_64
- Link to full system information report: [system_info.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16203089/system_info.txt)
- Proton version:   experimental-bleeding-edge-9.0

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

Game is unplayable/does not load in properly when starting new game with default Proton. With experimental-bleeding-edge-9.0 game loads in without issues and is fully playable, but the issue is that Text To Speech (TTS) feature is not working. 

**Note!** This issue can not be replicated with the demo of the game. The demo works on default Proton, but it lacks the TTS feature and looks and is functionally somewhat different.

## Reproduction
- Launch the game, notice how in the options menu TTS > customize it says "Voice Error"

![envelope1](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/857f8047-335e-452f-9dd0-63564b7324c2) ![envelope2](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4c087f59-aa33-4e4a-96c3-2b6535f487ba)

-  When you start a new game there is immediately some dialogue, note the speaker icon in the dialogue box. When pressing it, it should toggle between TTS and you should be hearing the machine speech

![envelope3](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/21615846-2dd8-40ab-baf6-302a0b0e83ad)

Proton Log:
[steam-2717480.experimental-bleeding-edge-9.0.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16203122/steam-2717480.experimental-bleeding-edge-9.0.log)

In the logs I see "\\system32\\Speech\\Common\\sapi.dll" so I guess the game is using Microsofts Speech API.

issue_title: The Dark Pictures Anthology: House of Ashes (1281590)
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: The Dark Pictures Anthology: House of Ashes
- Steam AppID of the game: 1281590

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 5700 
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 21.2.4 - kisak-mesa PPA
- Kernel version:  5.11.0-38-generic
- Proton version:  proton-6.3-7

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms 

With Proton 6.3-7 and current experimental, the game keeps asking to reconnect the controller (like every minute). Even though you are playing with a Keyboard & Mouse and never even had a controller connected in the first place. Other than that the game works fine if playing with a controller. 

This problem does not occur with Proton GE 6.19-GE-2

[steam-1281590.6.19-GE-2.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7401932/steam-1281590.6.19-GE-2.log)
[steam-1281590.experimental-6.3-20211020.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7401933/steam-1281590.experimental-6.3-20211020.log)
[steam-1281590.proton-6.3-7.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7401934/steam-1281590.proton-6.3-7.log)
[system_info.txt](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7401935/system_info.txt)

## Reproduction

- Make sure no controller is connected
- Launch the game
- When reaching the main menu, the game will start asking to reconnect a controller. And continues to do so even if you select to play with keyboard&mouse.

New World (1063730)

Issue transferred from https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/5774.
@hagenmt posted on 2022-04-19T03:54:17:

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: New World
- Steam AppID of the game: 1063730

## System Information
Distro: Ubuntu 20.04.4 LTS
Kernel: 5.13.0-39-generic
RAM:32 GB
GPU Driver:NVIDIA 510.54
GPU:NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 3GB
CPU:Intel Core i7-8700 @ 3.20GHz

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

log for experimental (not bleeding edge run):
[experimental-steam-1063730.zip](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/8509682/experimental-steam-1063730.zip)

log for 7.0.1:
[701-steam-1063730.zip](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/8509680/701-steam-1063730.zip)


## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Tried experimental, 7.0.1, and experimental bleeding edge.  It crashes about 2-5 minutes into playing. Early on I was able to play for quite a while, but not that I have about 4-6 hours into the game it crashes every time almost instantly.  I am sitting in the wilderness at this point just collecting some ore and hemp and it crashes usually about 3-4 resources nodes into collecting every time.

I only see this other New World issue reported: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/5772
But it appears to be more related to memory issues, I have 32GB of memory and I have always had a swap file as well so I don't think they are the same issue?

## Reproduction
Start steam
Launcher options: PROTON_LOG=1 bash -c 'exec "${@/NewWorldLauncher.exe/bin64\/NewWorld.exe}"' -- %command%
Hit Play
Select my character 
Search the near locations for some hemp or iron nodes and start mining
Crash after about 3-4 nodes
